name: Monad

phase_0.boundary_condition: The monad laws (left identity, right identity, associativity) that close and contain all valid monadic operations.

VINIT.justification: The void state before any monadic wrapping; the raw, unwrapped element that exists prior to monad instantiation.

TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor that contains and defines the entire monad structure; the boundary that makes M a complete, self-contained computational context.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: lifts a bare value into the monad; the directed transformation from unlifted to lifted state.

AREV.justification: Reverse morphism: unwraps the monad to recover the underlying value; descent from lifted back to unlifted.

CLINK.chosen_element: flatMap / bind composition (M a → (a → M b) → M b)
CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining of monadic operations; composition of two monadic computations into a single coherent flow.

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: Monad identity law: m >>= return ≡ m
IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference and self-recognition; the monad recognizes itself as itself after binding with return.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: Monadic branching: flatMap splits computation into two paths
FSPLIT.justification: One monadic computation branches into two distinct continuations based on the intermediate result; the fork point where a single M a becomes two possible M b paths.

FFUSE.chosen_element: Monadic fusion: associativity law (m >>= f) >>= g ≡ m >>= (λx. f x >>= g)
FFUSE.justification: Two sequential monadic binds reconstitute into a single unified bind; the branches rejoin into the original computation structure, preserving semantics.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch: the computation succeeds and produces a value; the true evaluation path within the monad.

EVALF.justification: Negative branch: the computation fails or produces no value; the false evaluation path, often representing error or absence.

ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: both success and failure held simultaneously without resolution; multiple possible outcomes coexist in superposition until extraction.

IFIX.justification: Permanent, irreversible record of a side effect; once a monadic action is executed, its effect is fixed and cannot be undone.

phase_2.split_element: flatMap: (M a) → (a → M b) → [M b path-1, M b path-2]
phase_2.split_input: A monadic value M a bound to a continuation function (a → M b)
split_outputs: Left branch: successful extraction of a, application of continuation, result M b
Right branch: alternative path or failure case, also yielding M b or error state
phase_2.fuse_element: Associativity: ((M a >>= f) >>= g) ≡ (M a >>= (λx. f x >>= g))
phase_2.fuse_result: A single unified monadic computation M c that semantically equals the original nested bind structure

phase_3.void_description: 00 — Uninitialized state; no monad instantiated, no value wrapped, no computational context established.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — Affirmative state; monad successfully contains a value, computation succeeded, continuation is valid.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — Negative state; monad contains failure/error, computation did not produce a value, continuation is blocked.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — Paradice state; monad simultaneously holds multiple possible outcomes (nondeterministic, probabilistic, or choice-based); both success and failure coexist until observation.

steps.domain_action: Unbound raw value a exists in void state before any monadic wrapping.
Monad type constructor M establishes the boundary that will contain all subsequent operations.
return lifts the raw value a into the monad, producing M a; forward morphism from unlifted to lifted.
flatMap branches the computation: M a is split into two paths — one for success (EVALT), one for failure (EVALF).
Success path: the value a is successfully extracted from M a and passed to the continuation function f.
Composition of f(a) with the next monadic operation; f(a) produces M b, chaining the computation forward.
Failure path: the monad contains an error or Nothing; the continuation is bypassed, failure propagates.
Associativity law reconstitutes the two branches back into a single unified monadic computation M c.
Identity law verified: m >>= return ≡ m; the monad recognizes itself after binding with return.
extract unwraps the final monad M c back to the underlying value c; reverse morphism from lifted to unlifted.
Nondeterministic monad enters paradice state: multiple outcomes held simultaneously without collapse.
Sequential composition of multiple monadic operations; each bind chains the next computation.
Monadic effect (e.g., IO action, state mutation, logging) is permanently recorded and cannot be undone.
Self-reference loop: the monad's output feeds back as input to verify consistency and closure.
Terminal anchor confirms the monad laws (left identity, right identity, associativity) are satisfied; system is closed and complete.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The monad type class definition and its bind (>>=) operator; translates sequential monadic intentions into chained computations.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Monadic bind (>>=) and return; components communicate by passing values through the monadic context.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The monad's internal state or wrapped value; stored within the monad constructor and retrieved via extract or pattern matching.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The order of bind operations; determined by the associativity law and the sequential chaining of flatMap calls.
phase_5.alfs_store: The monad laws themselves: left identity (return a >>= f ≡ f a), right identity (m >>= return ≡ m), and associativity ((m >>= f) >>= g ≡ m >>= (λx. f x >>= g)).

phase_6.cycle_cost: One monadic bind operation (flatMap); the cost of composing two computations into a single coherent flow.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Two separate monadic computations: M a and (a → M b), unbound and sequential.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: One unified monadic computation: M b, with the same semantic meaning but restructured via associativity.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The monad laws preserve information and structure; no entropy is generated because bind is a pure composition operation that reorganizes computation without loss or gain of semantic content.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → CLINK → EVALF → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → AREV → ENGAGR → CLINK → IFIX → IMSCRIB → TANCH
-- Class: Monad
-- Fingerprint: sig=(9,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=15
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 7)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [5] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [6] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [7] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [8] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑗  | identity — self-imscription
--   [9] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑠 → 𐑳  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [10] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑗 → 𐑱  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [11] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑳 → 𐑭  | composition — regime coherence
--   [12] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑱 → 𐑠  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [13] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑭 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [14] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def monad_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Unbound raw value a exists in void state before any monadic wrapping.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Monad type constructor M establishes the boundary that will contain all subse...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑚)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (return lifts the raw value a into the monad, producing M a; forward morphism ...)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (flatMap branches the computation: M a is split into two paths — one for success (EVALT), one for failure (EVALF).) / FFUSE [7] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Success path: the value a is successfully extracted from M a and passed to th...)
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Composition of f(a) with the next monadic operation; f(a) produces M b, chain...)
      -- F-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Failure path: the monad contains an error or Nothing; the continuation is byp...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [7]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [7] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Associativity law reconstitutes the two branches back into a single unified monadic computation M c.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [8] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Identity law verified: m >>= return ≡ m; the monad recognizes itself after bi...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑠 𐑳)  -- [9] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (extract unwraps the final monad M c back to the underlying value c; reverse m...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑗 𐑱)  -- [10] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Nondeterministic monad enters paradice state: multiple outcomes held simultan...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑳 𐑭)  -- [11] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Sequential composition of multiple monadic operations; each bind chains the n...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑠)  -- [12] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Monadic effect (e.g., IO action, state mutation, logging) is permanently reco...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑭 𐑡)  -- [13] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Self-reference loop: the monad's output feeds back as input to verify consist...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [14] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Terminal anchor confirms the monad laws (left identity, right identity, assoc...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def monad_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (monad_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def monad_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (monad_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem monad_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
